trade.summary: Germany exports machinery, vehicles, chemicals, and pharmaceuticals to the UAE. The UAE exports crude oil and re-exports goods. Post-2022 energy crisis, Germany accelerated LNG agreements with Gulf producers including the UAE, though volumes remain modest.

diplomatic.summary: Germany and the UAE maintain strong diplomatic relations supported by significant trade and investment ties. Germany has expressed concern over UAE human rights practices but maintains pragmatic engagement. The Gaza conflict (2023-24) strained some Arab relations with Western partners including Germany.

military.summary: Germany has sold defense equipment to the UAE including patrol vessels and communications systems, though German arms export policy sometimes restricts sales. Germany has no military bases in the UAE. Bundeswehr participated in Gulf security dialogues.

financial.summary: UAE sovereign wealth funds (ADIA, Mubadala) have significant German industrial and real estate investments. German companies (Siemens, BASF, Deutsche Bank) have major UAE operations. German-UAE investment corridor is well established.

energy.summary: Post-Russia energy shock, Germany signed LNG supply MoUs with ADNOC. RWE signed a gas agreement with the UAE in 2022. The UAE is becoming a more significant energy supplier to Germany as part of German energy diversification.

scientific.summary: German universities and research institutions collaborate with UAE counterparts in engineering, sustainability, and materials science. Fraunhofer and Max Planck institutes have UAE research partnerships.
